data.title: Trump threatens to strike Iranian power plants and bridges
data.description: US President Trump posted on social media that Tuesday will be both "Power Plant Day" and "Bridge Day," which will happen simultaneously in Iran.

data.title: Spark Completes First Month of Buyback Program
data.description: Spark has successfully completed its first month of buyback transactions, spending 571,957 USDS to purchase 26.66 million SPK tokens. According to Foresight News, the average price per SPK was $0.0215. This initial buyback represents approximately 1% of SPK's circulating supply. The buyback initiative is planned to continue for 12 months, with an estimated total buyback of 12% of the circulating supply, equating to around 320 million SPK.

data.title: Trump Announces Planned Strikes on Iranian Infrastructure
data.description: U.S. President Donald Trump announced on Truth Social plans to target Iranian power plants and bridges on Tuesday. According to BlockBeats, Trump described the day as 'Iran's power plant and bridge day, combined into one,' calling it an unprecedented move. He urged the opening of the strait, warning of severe consequences if not complied with.

data.title: Trump: F-15 pilot successfully rescued; press conference to be held Monday
data.description: US President Trump tweeted: "We have successfully rescued a seriously wounded and extremely brave F-15 crew member/officer from deep in the Iranian mountains. The Iranian military was conducting a massive search and had closed in. He is a highly respected colonel. Such raids are extremely rare due to the high risk to personnel and equipment, and almost never happen! The second operation followed the first, when we rescued the pilot in broad daylight, which was equally unusual, and we remained over Iranian airspace for seven hours. All those involved demonstrated incredible courage and capability! I will hold a press conference with the military in the Oval Office at 1 p.m. (local time) Monday (1 a.m. Beijing time the following day)."

data.title: Iran launches its 96th wave of strikes against the US and Israel
data.description: The Iranian Islamic Revolutionary Guard Corps (IRGC) issued a statement on May 5th, stating that its navy and aerospace forces launched the 96th wave of Operation True Commitment-4 in the early hours of the morning. The statement indicated that the IRGC launched a fierce attack on the Haifa oil refinery, which supplies fuel to Israeli warplanes, resulting in the destruction of its core components; it also used missiles to attack the natural gas facilities of ExxonMobil and Chevron in Habshan, UAE, and a petrochemical plant in Ruwais, UAE. Furthermore, multiple petrochemical facilities in Sitra, Bahrain, and Shuaiba, Kuwait, were also attacked by drones and missiles, causing complete shutdowns and large-scale fires. (CCTV News)

data.title: Samson Mow Warns Against Hasty Transition to Post-Quantum Signatures for Bitcoin
data.description: Samson Mow has cautioned against a rapid transition to post-quantum signatures for Bitcoin, highlighting potential drawbacks. According to NS3.AI, Mow noted that such a shift could increase signature sizes by 10 to 125 times, which would subsequently decrease network throughput. He emphasized that the threat posed by quantum computing is a medium- to long-term concern, potentially taking 10 to 20 years to become significant.

data.title: Iran's Revolutionary Guard Targets Facilities in Middle East
data.description: On April 5, Iran's Islamic Revolutionary Guard Corps announced strikes on petrochemical plants in the UAE, Kuwait, and Bahrain, as well as a refinery in Israel and a gas facility in the UAE. According to BlockBeats, the statement warned that any further attacks on civilian targets within Iran would lead to increased actions against U.S. economic interests in the region.

data.title: Iran says it has struck petrochemical and energy facilities in several Gulf states, warning of further damage to U.S. interests.
data.description: The Iranian Islamic Revolutionary Guard Corps issued a statement saying it had launched strikes against petrochemical plants in the UAE, Kuwait, and Bahrain, an oil refinery in Israel, and a gas facility in the UAE. Further attacks on civilian targets within Iran would further intensify the damage to US economic interests in the region. (Jinshi)

data.title: Planet Evening News
data.description: 1. Metaplanet responds to JPX new regulations consultation: Respects relevant procedures and will not affect the advancement of BTC strategy and ecosystem layout; 2. 10x Research: USDT issuance on Ethereum surpasses Tron, ETH may become the main beneficiary of stablecoin growth; 3. JAN3 CEO criticizes Bitcoin quantum security proposal as too hasty: Blind upgrades may trigger computational attack risks; 4. Oman and Iran hold talks: Discuss various solutions to ensure smooth passage of ships in the Strait of Hormuz; 5. An address deposited 1856 ETH into OKX, which would result in a loss of $1.89 million if sold; 6. Dragonfly transfers $2.12 million MNT to Bybit; 7. X product manager: Approximately 80% of cryptocurrency post activity is driven by bots; 8. Bitcoin social media bearish sentiment hits a 5-week high.

data.title: Political Themes Dominate 2026 Q1 Prediction Markets
data.description:
According to data from CryptoRank, political themes are expected to dominate the prediction markets in the first quarter of 2026. According to Foresight News, the largest trading volumes are seen in political topics, with sports themes holding a minimal share.

The top five predictions by trading volume include: '2026 Super Bowl Champion: Seattle Wins' with $704 million, 'Trump's Federal Reserve Chair Nomination: Kevin Warsh' at $617 million, 'March 2026 Federal Reserve Decision: No Change' with $260 million, 'U.S. Government Shutdown by January 31: Yes' at $157 million, and 'Portuguese Presidential Election: António José Seguro' with $136 million.

The data is sourced from CryptoRank.io and Polymarket. The probability of each prediction winning at the time of issuance was 0.8%, 34.0%, 57.5%, 28.5%, and 9.5%, respectively.

data.title: North Korean IT Workers Linked to Major Crypto Protocols Development
data.description:
North Korean IT workers have reportedly been involved in the development of several well-known cryptocurrency protocols, according to Foresight News. The projects trace back to the DeFi Summer period and include SushiSwap, THORChain, Harmony, Ankr, Shib, and Yearn. 

The investigation, shared by on-chain investigator @tayvano_, reveals that these workers have accurately listed '7 years of blockchain development experience' on their resumes, indicating their substantial involvement in the industry.

data.title: JAN3 CEO criticizes Bitcoin quantum security proposal as rushed: blind upgrades could introduce computational attack risks.
data.description: JAN3 CEO Samson Mow stated in an article on the X platform that the current response to the threat of Bitcoin quantum computing (QC) should not be rushed. A hasty switch from existing signature mechanisms to quantum-resistant (PQ) schemes could expose Bitcoin to classical computing attacks in the short term. PQ signatures could increase size by 10 to 125 times, significantly reducing network throughput and potentially triggering a repeat of the scaling controversies of the early "block wars." Furthermore, Mow warned of potential backdoor risks in some PQ schemes. The quantum computing threat is still in the medium to long term (potentially 10 to 20 years), and the more reasonable approach now is continuous research rather than hasty deployment. He also specifically named Coinbase, stating that its wallet has a quantum attack vulnerability due to address reuse and suggesting that fixing related infrastructure issues should be prioritized.

data.title: Visa Executives Highlight South Korea as Prime Location for Stablecoin Trials
data.description: Visa Group President Oliver Jenkins and Asia-Pacific Business Head Steven Carpin have identified South Korea as an ideal location for stablecoin experimentation. According to Foresight News, during their visit to several banks in South Korea, Jenkins noted the country's significant number of cryptocurrency investors, totaling 17 million. He also highlighted South Korea's high subscription rate to generative AI platforms like ChatGPT, ranking second only to the United States. Jenkins emphasized that South Korea could potentially lead the integration of AI and stablecoin businesses in the future, making it a crucial market outside the U.S. for such trials.

data.title: Bitcoin Founder Satoshi Nakamoto's Presumed Birthday Coincides with Historical Gold Ban Anniversary
data.description: April 5 marks the presumed 51st birthday of Bitcoin founder Satoshi Nakamoto. According to Foresight News, Nakamoto set his birthday as April 5, 1975, on the P2P Foundation platform in 2009. This date coincides with the anniversary of former U.S. President Roosevelt's Executive Order 6102 issued in 1933, which prohibited private ownership of gold. The order is seen as a metaphor for Nakamoto's stance on decentralized, anti-government monopoly currency.

data.title: Ethereum's Potential Upside Linked to Stablecoin Growth
data.description: Ethereum may see benefits from the growth of stablecoins, according to 10x Research. The issuance of USDT on Ethereum has recently surpassed that on Tron, with ETH currently trading near $2,000 following a 57% decline from its August 2025 peak. According to NS3.AI, the firm noted that weak on-chain activity has constrained ETH demand since November, prompting a reassessment of whether ETH is approaching a critical turning point.

data.title: Oil Tanker Transits Strait of Hormuz Following Iranian Exemption Announcement
data.description: An oil tanker carrying Iraqi crude has been observed navigating the Strait of Hormuz near the Iranian coast, a day after Iran announced Iraq's exemption from restrictions on this vital passage. According to Odaily, data from LSEG and Kpler indicate that the vessel, named 'Ocean Thunder,' loaded approximately one million barrels of Basra heavy crude on March 2. It is expected to unload in Pengerang, Malaysia, by mid-April.

data.title: Iranian media reports that several US soldiers died during the operation to rescue the pilot.
data.description: According to Iran's Fars News Agency on the 5th, citing sources, several US soldiers died during the US military's operation to rescue the pilot of the downed fighter jet. The sources also said that the US military attempted to destroy the wreckage of the downed aircraft and even the bodies of the fallen US soldiers. (Xinhua)

data.title: Oil tankers carrying Iraqi crude oil pass through the Strait of Hormuz
data.description: Data from LSEG and Kpler shows that a tanker carrying Iraqi crude oil was spotted transiting the Strait of Hormuz along a route close to the Iranian coast, a day after Iran indicated that Iraq could be exempted from restrictions on passage through this crucial shipping lane. Kpler data shows that the "Ocean Thunder" loaded approximately 1 million barrels of Basra Heavy crude oil on March 2nd and is expected to unload in Pengerang, Malaysia, in mid-April. (Jinshi)

data.title: 10x Research: USDT issuance on Ethereum surpasses Tron; ETH may become a major beneficiary of stablecoin growth.
data.description: 10x Research published an article on the X platform stating that ETH's performance over the past five years has been lackluster, with its price hovering around the $2,000 level reached in the previous cycle. Since November, 10x Research has maintained a cautious stance due to sluggish on-chain activity limiting ETH demand and value accumulation. After a 57% drop from its August 2025 high, ETH is currently relatively undervalued, compared to a roughly 42% drop in BTC over the same period. Despite facing significant market capitalization losses, such as the approximately $8 billion drawdown by ETH treasury companies like Bitmine, capital accumulation continues, with USDT issuance on the Ethereum network recently surpassing Tron. This has fueled the view that Ethereum could become a major beneficiary of stablecoin growth and a potential backbone of Wall Street-driven on-chain infrastructure finance. 10x Research is reassessing whether ETH is nearing a turning point or whether structural resistance still exists.

data.title: Iranian Speaker Criticizes U.S. Victory Claims
data.description: Iranian Parliament Speaker Mohammad Bagher Ghalibaf criticized the United States on social media, sharing an image of aircraft wreckage. According to Odaily, Ghalibaf commented that if the U.S. continues to achieve such 'victories,' it will face complete destruction. He stated, 'If the U.S. achieves three more such victories, it will be utterly destroyed.' Iranian media, including the Iranian Students' News Agency, previously shared the same image, identifying it as the wreckage of a U.S. military aircraft involved in a rescue operation. Some Iranian outlets suggest Ghalibaf's post mocks U.S. President Donald Trump's claims of 'imaginary victories.'
